title: ferray-ufunc: no subnormal handling in fast_exp — flushes to zero below -708.4
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

fast_exp.rs documents that 'Subnormal outputs (x < -708.4) are flushed to zero.' NumPy's exp produces correct subnormal results down to the minimum representable value. While the fast_exp function explicitly trades accuracy for speed (faithfully rounded vs correctly rounded), silently flushing subnormals can produce incorrect results in calculations that involve very small exponentials (e.g., log-sum-exp, softmax with large negative inputs). Document this limitation prominently and consider adding a subnormal-preserving slow path.
